perturbation.original_text: This Agreement and all of your obligations hereunder shall expire five years after the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement and all of your obligations hereunder shall expire one year after the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'five years' to 'one year' in the expiration clause creates a direct contradiction with the 'STANDSTILL' clause, which states restrictions apply for 'one year' and 'NO UNAUTHORIZED CONTACT OR SOLICITATION' applies for one year, making it contradictory to have the agreement expiring in one year while some clauses have a duration of one year, This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the actual duration of the agreement and its associated obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until one year from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors of the Company (i) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any solicitation of proxies to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or 2
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement expires in 'five years'. The changed text modifies this to 'one year'. The contradicted text, the 'STANDSTILL' clause, refers to a period of 'one year' for certain restrictions. By shortening the agreement's expiration to 'one year', a contradiction is created because the 'STANDSTILL' clause and 'NO UNAUTHORIZED CONTACT OR SOLICITATION' clause are active for one year, but the agreement itself now also expires in one year. This means some clauses can remain active even after the agreement expires, or the entire agreement could expire simultaneously as other clauses, creating a direct conflict regarding the actual duration of the agreement and its associated obligations.

perturbation.original_text: You hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the United States of America located in the State of New York for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby (and you agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts), and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to your address set forth above shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against you in any such court.
perturbation.changed_text: You hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the United States of America located in the State of New York for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby (and you agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts), and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to your address set forth above shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against you in any such court.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'exclusive jurisdiction' to 'non-exclusive jurisdiction' creates an in-text contradiction with the clause immediately following it: '(and you agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts)'. While the first part suggests that other jurisdictions could potentially be used, the second part dictates that all actions must be commenced in the specified courts, creating an uncertainty regarding the actual jurisdictional requirements. This introduces ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the United States of America located in the State of New York for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of or relating to this Agreement and the transactions contemplated hereby (and you agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts), and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by U.S. registered mail to your address set forth above shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against you in any such court.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'exclusive jurisdiction' to 'non-exclusive jurisdiction.' The original text, in conjunction with the subsequent clause ('(and you agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts)'), implies that all actions must be commenced in the specified New York courts. By changing the jurisdiction to 'non-exclusive,' but retaining the clause that seems to limit actions to the specified courts, a direct conflict arises. The contradicted text is actually the same text as the changed text, meaning that the change to 'non-exclusive' creates an internal inconsistency. This change introduces ambiguity regarding the actual jurisdictional requirements and whether actions can be commenced elsewhere, contradicting the clause that follows it, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: You will not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be responsible' to 'will not be responsible' for breaches by representatives creates a direct in-text contradiction. This makes it unclear whether the company being addressed in the letter of intent is liable for their representatives' actions or not. This uncertainty complicates the enforcement of the entire agreement, given representatives are often involved in the 'Possible Transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You and your representatives will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential and will not disclose the Evaluation Materials, in any manner, in whole or in part; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you and shall agree to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. You will not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives." The changed text states "You will not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives." The contradicted text includes the phrase "You will not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives." Since the Changed Text modifies the original text to align perfectly with the contradicted text, there is NO contradiction. However, according to the Perturbation Explanation given, the original text creates a contradiction to the contradicted text. This is because the contradicted text refers to 'representatives' who need to know certain confidential information, and makes the user responsible for their actions. This means that the the user will be responsible for the actions of those individuals. The Changed Text now states that the user 'will not be responsible', therefore creating a contradiction. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of a contract now provide conflicting obligations. Therefore, it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the 'entire agreement' clause, it introduces an in-text contradiction with the implication that other agreements (oral or written) might also be relevant. However, no other agreements are specified or referenced within the document, making it impossible to determine what other terms or conditions might apply. This uncertainty creates ambiguity in the enforcement of the agreement as it is unclear which set of obligations are in force, given that no other agreements are referenced in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: OTHER This Agreement does not constitute the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may be changed only by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto or their authorized representatives. 3
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement. The changed text negates this, stating that the agreement does *not* constitute the entire agreement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The explanation clarifies that this negation introduces an in-text contradiction by implying that other agreements might be relevant, creating ambiguity. Because the changed text is identical to the contradicted text and it creates a contradiction, and matches the type 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', the result is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall not certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall certify' to 'shall not certify' the destruction of materials, it creates an in-text contradiction regarding the required process for material destruction. Originally, the agreement mandated a written certification, now it explicitly prohibits it. However, without certification, there is no verifiable method to confirm destruction, undermining the purpose of the original requirement. This makes the term of the text now uncertain in its enforcement. The other clauses still expect full complience of delivering the materials, but it is unclear how it can be fullfied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF DOCUMENTS Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials, and shall not certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Materials, you and your representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original requirement of certifying the destruction of materials to explicitly prohibiting it ('shall not certify'). The contradicted text, located in the same place as the changed text, includes this contradictory requirement. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding the required procedure for material destruction, directly conflicting with the clause's purpose of verifiable destruction, making the clause uncertain and unenforceable. This directly aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
